content: Naive pairwise (all-pairs) proximity/collision checking is O(n^2) — 100 entities already requires ~5000 pairwise checks — and becomes a real bottleneck well before large n. Standard fix is a broad-phase spatial partition (uniform grid, quadtree, or simple sorted-axis sweep) that cheaply narrows candidate pairs before an exact narrow-phase check; a spatial grid degrades to brute-force performance only in pathological worst cases and is otherwise substantially faster.
